1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

1	# Behavioral spec inferred from the oracle (wellformedness checker)
2	
3	All facts below are derived from oracle observation (probes in
4	scratchpad/probes, captures in oracle/captures). Nothing here is taken from
5	tamarin-prover source.
6	
7	## Overall report shape
8	
9	The oracle emits the wellformedness result inside the pretty-printed theory,
10	wrapped in a block comment.
11	
12	Success (empty report):
13	
14	    /* All wellformedness checks were successful. */
15	
16	Failure (>=1 topic):
17	
18	    /*
19	    WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!
20	
21	    <block1>
22	
23	    <block2>
24	    ...
25	    */
26	
27	Inner text (what `render_report` produces for a non-empty report) is:
28	
29	    "WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!" + "\n\n" + join(blocks, "\n\n")
30	
31	Each `block` = topic header + "\n" + underline + "\n\n" + body, where
32	`underline` = '=' repeated char_count(topic) (topic length INCLUDING any
33	leading/trailing space). Bodies may themselves contain blank lines.
34	
35	`render_report` for the empty report returns the success line
36	`/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */`.
37	`underline_topic(t)` returns `t + "\n" + "=".repeat(char_count(t))`.
38	
39	## Canonical topic order (report order)
40	
41	Observed by kitchen-sink probes ks1 / ks3 and the round-2 ordering probes
42	(comb1, ord_tail, mega_tail, diff_mega, diff_place):
43	
44	 1. `Unbound variables`
45	 2. `Fresh public constants`                             (round2; always-on)
46	 3. `Public constants with mismatching capitalization`   (public-names report)
47	 4. `Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization`
48	 5. `Reserved names`
49	 5a. `Reserved prefixes`                                 (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
50	 6. `Fr facts must only use a fresh- or a msg-variable`
51	 7. `Special facts`
52	 8. `Fact arity issues`                                  (non-diff; diff merges these
53	 9. `Fact multiplicity issues`                            two into "Fact usage" - OUT OF SCOPE)
54	10. `Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side ` (TRAILING space)
55	10a. `Left rule` / `Right rule`                          (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
56	11. `Formula terms`
57	12. ` Formula guardedness`   (LEADING space)
58	13. `Lemma annotations`                                  (round2; always-on)
59	14. `Multiplication restriction of rules`                (round2; always-on)
60	15. `Nat Sorts`
61	16. `Subterm Convergence Warning`
62	17. `Message Derivation Checks` (appended by binary; needs Maude - OUT OF SCOPE)
63	    / `Derivation Checks` (timeout variant - OUT OF SCOPE)
64	
65	Round-2 ordering evidence: comb1 -> `Unbound, Fresh public constants,
66	Public names, mismatching sorts, Reserved`; mega_tail -> `Formula terms,
67	Formula guardedness, Lemma annotations, Multiplication restriction, Nat Sorts,
68	Subterm`; diff_mega/diff_place -> `... Reserved names, Reserved prefixes,
69	Fr facts, ... lhs-not-rhs, Left/Right rule, Formula terms, ...`.
70	
71	Public names (#3) is generated separately and *inserted before* the first of a
72	set of anchor topics = every topic from #4 (mismatching sorts) onward.
73	`after_public_names_topics()` returns that anchor list; `insert_wf_before(
74	report, errs, anchors)` inserts `errs` immediately before the first report
75	entry whose topic is in `anchors` (appends at end if none present). Unbound
76	variables (#1) and Fresh public constants (#2) are emitted before the
77	public-names insertion point (so they are NOT anchors).
78	
79	### Diff-mode-only vs. always-on
80	
81	Gated on `theory.is_diff`: `Reserved prefixes`, `Left rule`, `Right rule`
82	(observed silent in non-diff mode; diff_reserved_prefix without `--diff` emits
83	nothing). Always-on (both modes): `Fresh public constants`, `Lemma
84	annotations`, `Multiplication restriction of rules`.
85	
86	## Per-check triggers and message templates
87	
88	### Unbound variables
89	Trigger: a protocol rule uses a variable in actions/conclusions not bound by a
90	premise. Entries joined by "\n  \n":
91	    "  rule `NAME' has unbound variables: \n    v1, v2"
92	(trailing space after "variables:"). Vars sorted, ", " joined, term-printed.
93	
94	### Public constants with mismatching capitalization (public_names_report)
95	Trigger: two public constants whose names are equal under ASCII-lowercasing but
96	differ in capitalization. Body:
97	    "Identifiers are case-sensitive, mismatched capitalizations are considered as different, i.e., 'ID' is different from 'id'. Check the capitalization of your identifiers.\n\n" + numbered items
98	Each lowercased-key group -> one item "  N. " + occurrences. Occurrences group by
99	location; same loc: `rule "R1":  name 'Alice', 'alice'`; different locs joined
100	", ": `rule "R1":  name 'Server', rule "R2":  name 'server'`. Names ASCII-sorted.
101	`public_names_report_from_pairs` takes raw (location, name) pairs.
102	
103	### Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization
104	Trigger: within one rule, two variables share a base name but differ in sort
105	prefix ($x/x/~x) or capitalization. Fixed header:
106	    "Possible reasons:\n1. Identifiers are case sensitive, i.e.,'x' and 'X' are considered to be different.\n2. The same holds for sorts:, i.e., '$x', 'x', and '~x' are considered to be different.\n"
107	per rule: "  rule `A5': \n    1. $s, ~s" (trailing space; numbered variant groups).
108	
109	### Reserved names
110	Trigger: rule uses a fact with a reserved name in a position. The reserved set
111	is POSITION-DEPENDENT (observed z9/z11/z12):
112	  - left-hand-side / right-hand-side: {K, KU, KD}
113	  - the middle (actions): {K, KU, KD, In, Out, Fr}  (the I/O facts are also
114	    reserved as actions; on LHS/RHS they are "Special facts" instead)
115	Per (rule, position) entry joined "\n  \n":
116	    "  Rule `R1' contains facts with reserved names on left-hand-side:\n    <facts>"
117	Position phrases: LHS `on left-hand-side:`, actions `on the middle:`,
118	RHS `on the right-hand-side:`. Facts via fact printer, fsep comma-joined.
119	
120	### Fr facts must only use a fresh- or a msg-variable
121	Trigger: `Fr(t)` where t is not fresh/msg var. Entries joined "\n  \n":
122	    "  rule `R1' fact: Fr( $p )"
123	
124	### Special facts
125	Trigger: reserved I/O fact in disallowed position (Out/Fr/K in premises,
126	In/K in conclusions). Per (rule, side) joined "\n  \n":
127	    "  rule `R1' uses disallowed facts on left-hand-side:\n    Out( x )"
128	(sides `on left-hand-side:`/`on right-hand-side:`; lowercase "rule").
129	
130	### Fact arity issues
131	Trigger: same fact name with >1 arity. Body:
132	    "Same fact is used with different arities, i.e., Fact('A','B') is different from Fact('A'). \nCheck the arguments of your facts.\n  " + factBlocks
133	facts sorted by lowercased name; factBlock (each prefixed "\n"):
134	    "\n  Fact `foo':\n\n" + items + "\n  "
135	item i: "    {i}. Rule `R', arity {n}\n         {factPP}"; items joined "\n    \n".
136	Lemma-sourced facts render as raw Haskell `Fact {factTag = ...}` (gap).
137	
138	### Fact multiplicity issues
139	Same structure keyed on multiplicity; header:
140	    "Same fact is used with different multiplicities, i.e., !Fact() (Persistent fact) exists along with Fact() (Linear) in your rules. \nCheck the multiplicity (persistence) of your facts.\n  " + factBlocks
141	item i: "    {i}. Rule `R', multiplicity (persistence) {Linear|Persistent}\n         {factPP}".
142	
143	### Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side  (fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs)
144	Trigger: user fact identity (name,arity,mult) in some premise, never in any
145	conclusion. Numbered items joined "\n  \n":
146	    "  1. in rule \"r1\":  factName `X' arity: 0 multiplicity: Linear" +
147	      optional ". Perhaps you want to use the fact in rule \"r1\":  factName `Y' arity: 0 multiplicity: Linear"
148	suggestion = nearest RHS fact by edit distance under a threshold (omitted when
149	nothing close). Special facts excluded from both sides.
150	
151	### Fresh public constants (round2; always-on)
152	Trigger: a fresh-name literal (`~'foo'`, AST `Term::FreshLit`) used directly in
153	a rule. Constants collected in the order premises, conclusions, actions
154	(probe fpc_positions). Per rule with >=1 hit, entries joined "\n  \n":
155	    "  rule `NAME': fresh public constants are not allowed: ~'foo', ~'bar'"
156	constants rendered by the term printer, ", " joined. GAP: for a long constant
157	list the oracle word-wraps with a 4-space continuation indent (fpc_positions);
158	implementation emits a single line, so byte-parity fixtures use short lists.
159	
160	### Reserved prefixes (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
161	Trigger (diff theories only): a fact whose name starts with `DiffIntr` or
162	`DiffProto`. Facts collected in the order premises, actions, conclusions
163	(probe rp_multi). Per rule the body is:
164	    "  The Rule `NAME' contains facts with reserved prefixes ('DiffIntr',\n  'DiffProto') inside names:\n  \n" + factblocks
165	The HEADER is word-filled (Wadler `fillSep`) at WIDTH 69, indent 2 (measured via
166	rp_long/rp_med/rp_w47: a line breaks before the next word once column would
167	exceed 69). Words treat the rule name `\`NAME'` and each of `('DiffIntr',` /
168	`'DiffProto')` as single tokens. Each factblock (joined "\n  \n"):
169	    "    <fact_pp>\n    (ProtoFact <Mult> \"<name>\" <arity>,<arity>,<Mult>)"
170	The 2nd line is a raw Haskell Show of a (FactTag, arity, Multiplicity) tuple;
171	<Mult> is `Linear`/`Persistent`. GAP: multi-RULE joining unprobed (fixtures use
172	one rule).
173	
174	### Left rule / Right rule (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
175	Trigger (diff theories only): a diff rule (`Rule.left_right = Some((l,r))`)
176	whose explicit `left`/`right` projection differs from the parent rule projected
177	to that side (`diff(a,b)` -> a on left, -> b on right). Per rule the LEFT
178	projection is tested first; if inconsistent the RIGHT is not reported for that
179	rule (probe diff_both: both sides bad -> only "Left rule"). Body per entry
180	(joined "\n  \n"):
181	    "  Inconsistent left rule\n" + indent4(explicit_rule_pp) +
182	    "\n  \n  w.r.t.\n  \n" + indent4(parent_rule_pp)
183	("left"/"right" and topic "Left rule"/"Right rule"). Consistency compared via
184	the projected vs. explicit fact lists. Left rule topic precedes Right rule.
185	
186	### Lemma annotations (round2; always-on)
187	Trigger: a lemma with attribute `reuse` AND quantifier `exists-trace` (reuse on
188	`all-traces` is fine - probe la_alltraces). Entries joined "\n  \n":
189	    "  Lemma `NAME': cannot reuse 'exists-trace' lemmas"
190	(Topic may host other annotation conflicts not yet observed.)
191	
192	### Multiplication restriction of rules (round2; always-on)
193	Trigger: a rule whose CONCLUSIONS contain a multiplication (`*`, AST
194	`BinOp::Mult`) term. `*` only in premises or only in actions does NOT trigger
195	(probes mul_multi, mul_act); a `*` in a premise is not listed even when the
196	conclusion also has one (mul_pc). Per rule, entry (joined "\n  \n"):
197	    "  The following rule is not multiplication restricted:\n" + indent4(rule_pp) +
198	    "\n  \n  After replacing reducible function symbols in lhs with variables:\n" + indent4(rule2_pp) +
199	    "\n  \n    Terms with multiplication:  <terms>"
200	<terms> = maximal `*`-subterms of the conclusions, ", " joined (mul_terms).
201	GAPS: (a) rule2 (LHS reducible symbols replaced by fresh vars) is rendered equal
202	to rule1 - correct only when the LHS has no reducible symbols (probe mul_exp
203	shows `g^c` -> `x.1` otherwise); (b) the alternate failure mode
204	"Variables that occur only in rhs:  <vars>" (mul_exp) is not implemented; (c)
205	the co-emitted `Message Derivation Checks` block is Maude-derived, OUT OF SCOPE;
206	(d) rule_pp wrapping for long rules (see printer note).
207	
208	### Formula terms
209	Trigger: lemma/restriction uses a term that is not a public constant nor a bound
210	node/message variable (free var or reducible function). Body:
211	    "  Lemma `L1' uses terms of the wrong form: `Free y'\n  \n" + fixed_help
212	Short single term -> inline; longer/complex -> indented next line
213	`    `t1', `t2'` (reducible terms use de Bruijn `Bound N` - gap).
214	fixed_help = "  The only allowed terms are public constants and bound node and\n  message variables. If you encounter free message variables, then\n  you might have forgotten a #-prefix. Sort prefixes can only be\n  dropped where this is unambiguous. Moreover, reducible function\n  symbols are disallowed."
215	Entity label `Lemma` or `Restriction`.
216	
217	### Formula guardedness (topic has LEADING space)
218	Trigger: a LEMMA formula not convertible to guarded form. (An unguarded
219	RESTRICTION is instead a FATAL error, not a warning -- observed z1; so this
220	check applies to lemmas only.) Body:
221	    "  Lemma `L2' cannot be converted to a guarded formula:\n    unguarded variable(s) '#j' in the subformula\n      \"<pp>\"\n    in the formula\n      \"<pp>\""
222	<pp> = unicode formula pretty-print (full printer + guardedness algorithm partial - gap).
223	
224	### Nat Sorts
225	Trigger: var used in %+ (nat) context not of sort nat. Entries joined "\n  \n":
226	    "  y in term (y%+z) must be of sort nat" (nat-sort inference - structural only, gap).
227	
228	### Subterm Convergence Warning
229	Trigger: user equation l=r not subterm convergent. Body:
230	    "  User-defined equations must be convergent and have the finite variant property. The following equations are not subterm convergent. If you are sure that the set of equations is nevertheless convergent and has the finite variant property, you can ignore this warning and continue \n\n" + eqs + "\n   \n For more information, please refer to the manual : https://tamarin-prover.com/manual/master/book/010_modeling-issues.html "
231	each eq "    {lhsPP} = {rhsPP}" (subterm decision + printer partial - gap).
232	
233	## Term pretty-printer (observed)
234	- Var: sortprefix + base + ("."+idx if idx>0). Fresh `~`, Pub `$`, Nat `%`,
235	  Node `#`, Msg/Untagged ``.
236	- Pair / App("pair",[a,b]): `<a, b, ...>` (right-nested pairs flattened).
237	- App(f,args): `f(a, b)` (nullary -> `f`).
238	- PubLit s: `'s'`.
239	- BinOp: Exp `a^b` (no outer parens); Mult `(a*b)`; Union `(a++b)`;
240	  Xor `(a(+)b)` U+2295 AC-sorted; NatPlus `(a%+b)`.
241	- NatOne -> `%1`.
242	- Fact: [`!` if persistent] + name + "(" + (" "+args.join(", ")+" )" if args else " )").
243	
244	## Rule pretty-printer (observed; round2)
245	Used by Left/Right rule and Multiplication restriction. Base form (single line
246	when short):
247	    "rule (modulo E) NAME:\n   [ prems ] <arrow> [ concls ]"
248	- 3-space indent before the fact-list line; fact lists `[ f1, f2 ]`, `[ ]` empty.
249	- <arrow> = `-->` (no actions) or `--[ acts ]->` (actions present).
250	- `(modulo E)` (the AST modulo field, default "E").
251	- When embedded in a warning the whole 2-line block is indented (4 for
252	  Left/Right and Multiplication, giving 4 and 4+3=7 spaces).
253	GAP: long rules wrap - the arrow drops to its own line indented 6 and the
254	conclusions to indent 7 (probe mul_terms). Byte-parity fixtures use short rules.
255	
256	## Formula variable identity (round2 fix)
257	For free-variable detection (Formula terms) and guardedness the oracle treats a
258	quantified message variable and its occurrences as ONE variable even when the
259	parsed AST tags them with different-but-compatible sorts (Msg vs the Untagged
260	default; a temporal written `@ i` vs a quantifier `#i`). Matching on the full
261	sort tag produced spurious "Formula terms"/" Formula guardedness" reports on
262	round2/exists_trace_reuse (the integration bug). FIX: bind/compare formula
263	variables by NAME only. All prior fixtures (p05, p21, f_subterm) use consistent
264	sorts and are unaffected.

268	  translation; not derivable from the AST. Not produced by check_theory.
269	- AC operator argument ordering (xor/multiset) needs the term ordering.
270	- de Bruijn `Bound N` term rendering + reducible detection in Formula terms /
271	  raw lemma-fact rendering in arity/multiplicity: partial.
272	- Full unicode formula pretty-printer + guardedness algorithm: partial.
273	- Nat-sort inference / subterm-convergence decision: structural scaffolding only.

1	//! wf-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover wellformedness
2	//! checker, derived purely from black-box oracle behavior. See
3	//! workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the inferred behavioral spec.
4	
5	pub mod ast;
6	pub mod checks;
7	pub mod formula;
8	pub mod pretty;
9	pub mod report;
10	
11	pub use ast::*;
12	pub use report::{
13	    insert_wf_before, render_report, topics, underline_topic, WfError, WfReport, SUCCESS_LINE,
14	};
15	
16	/// Topics that follow the public-names report in the canonical order; the
17	/// public-names report is inserted immediately before the first of these that
18	/// is present in the report. (Unbound variables and Fresh public constants
19	/// precede the insertion point and are therefore NOT anchors.)
20	pub fn after_public_names_topics() -> Vec<&'static str> {
21	    vec![
22	        checks::T_SORTS,
23	        checks::T_RESERVED,
24	        checks::T_RESERVED_PREFIX,
25	        checks::T_FR,
26	        checks::T_SPECIAL,
27	        checks::T_ARITY,
28	        checks::T_MULT,
29	        checks::T_LHSRHS,
30	        checks::T_LEFT,
31	        checks::T_RIGHT,
32	        checks::T_FORMULA_TERMS,
33	        checks::T_GUARD,
34	        checks::T_LEMMA_ANNOT,
35	        checks::T_MULRESTRICT,
36	        checks::T_NAT,
37	        checks::T_SUBTERM,
38	    ]
39	}
40	
41	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order.
42	pub fn check_theory(thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
43	    let mut report: WfReport = Vec::new();
44	    // Base pipeline (public names inserted afterwards at its anchor point).
45	    report.extend(checks::unbound_variables(thy)); // 1
46	    report.extend(checks::fresh_public_constants(thy)); // 2
47	    report.extend(checks::mismatching_sorts(thy)); // 4
48	    report.extend(checks::reserved_names(thy)); // 5
49	    report.extend(checks::reserved_prefixes(thy)); // 6 (diff mode only)
50	    report.extend(checks::fr_facts(thy)); // 7
51	    report.extend(checks::special_facts(thy)); // 8
52	    report.extend(checks::fact_arity(thy)); // 9
53	    report.extend(checks::fact_multiplicity(thy)); // 10
54	    report.extend(checks::fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs(thy)); // 11
55	    report.extend(checks::diff_left_right(thy)); // 12, 13 (diff mode only)
56	    report.extend(checks::formula_terms(thy)); // 14
57	    report.extend(checks::formula_guardedness(thy)); // 15
58	    report.extend(checks::lemma_annotations(thy)); // 16
59	    report.extend(checks::multiplication_restriction(thy)); // 17
60	    report.extend(checks::nat_sorts(thy)); // 18
61	    report.extend(checks::subterm_convergence(thy)); // 19
62	
63	    let pn = checks::public_names_report(thy); // 3
64	    let anchors = after_public_names_topics();
65	    insert_wf_before(&mut report, pn, &anchors);
66	    report
67	}
68	
69	/// Secondary entry point: report any of `lemma_names` that are not declared in
70	/// the theory. NOTE: this entry point's exact render string could not be
71	/// observed through the file oracle; the set-difference logic is verified but
72	/// the message text is a documented gap (see BEHAVIOR.md).
73	pub fn check_if_lemmas_in_theory(lemma_names: &[String], thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
74	    let present = checks::theory_lemma_names(thy);
75	    let mut out = Vec::new();
76	    for name in lemma_names {
77	        if !present.contains(name) {
78	            out.push(WfError::new(
79	                "Lemmas",
80	                format!("lemma `{}' referenced but not present in theory", name),
81	            ));
82	        }
83	    }
84	    out
85	}
86	
87	// Re-export the public secondary entry points named in required_api.md.
88	pub use checks::{
89	    fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs, public_names_report, public_names_report_from_pairs,
90	};

1	//! Single-line unicode pretty-printer for trace formulas, calibrated from the
2	//! oracle (e.g. ks1 lemma L2 -> "∀ x #i #j. (Act( x ) @ #i) ⇒ (x = x)").
3	//! Wide formulas in the oracle wrap across multiple indented lines; this
4	//! printer emits a single line and is therefore exact only for formulas that
5	//! the oracle also keeps on one line (see BEHAVIOR.md gaps).
6	
7	use crate::ast::*;
8	use crate::pretty::{pp_fact, pp_term};
9	
10	pub fn pp_formula(f: &Formula) -> String {
11	    match f {
12	        Formula::False => "⊥".to_string(),
13	        Formula::True => "⊤".to_string(),
14	        Formula::Atom(a) => pp_atom(a),
15	        Formula::Not(g) => format!("¬({})", pp_formula(g)),
16	        Formula::And(a, b) => format!("({}) ∧ ({})", pp_formula(a), pp_formula(b)),
17	        Formula::Or(a, b) => format!("({}) ∨ ({})", pp_formula(a), pp_formula(b)),
18	        Formula::Implies(a, b) => format!("({}) ⇒ ({})", pp_formula(a), pp_formula(b)),
19	        Formula::Iff(a, b) => format!("({}) ⇔ ({})", pp_formula(a), pp_formula(b)),
20	        Formula::Forall(vs, g) => format!("∀ {}. {}", pp_bound_vars(vs), pp_formula(g)),
21	        Formula::Exists(vs, g) => format!("∃ {}. {}", pp_bound_vars(vs), pp_formula(g)),
22	    }
23	}
24	
25	fn pp_bound_vars(vs: &[VarSpec]) -> String {
26	    vs.iter()
27	        .map(crate::pretty::pp_var)
28	        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
29	        .join(" ")
30	}
31	
32	fn pp_atom(a: &Atom) -> String {
33	    match a {
34	        Atom::Eq(x, y) => format!("{} = {}", pp_term(x), pp_term(y)),
35	        Atom::Less(x, y) => format!("{} < {}", pp_term(x), pp_term(y)),
36	        Atom::LessMset(x, y) => format!("{} ⋖ {}", pp_term(x), pp_term(y)),
37	        Atom::Subterm(x, y) => format!("{} ⊏ {}", pp_term(x), pp_term(y)),
38	        Atom::Action(f, t) => format!("{} @ {}", pp_fact(f), pp_term(t)),
39	        Atom::Last(t) => format!("last({})", pp_term(t)),
40	        Atom::Pred(f) => pp_fact(f),
41	    }
42	}

/// Run every check in the oracle's report order.
pub fn check_theory(thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
    let mut report: WfReport = Vec::new();
    // Base pipeline (public names inserted afterwards at its anchor point).
    report.extend(checks::unbound_variables(thy)); // 1
    report.extend(checks::fresh_public_constants(thy)); // 2
    report.extend(checks::mismatching_sorts(thy)); // 4
    report.extend(checks::reserved_names(thy)); // 5
    report.extend(checks::reserved_prefixes(thy)); // 6 (diff mode only)
    report.extend(checks::fr_facts(thy)); // 7
    report.extend(checks::special_facts(thy)); // 8
    report.extend(checks::fact_arity(thy)); // 9
    report.extend(checks::fact_multiplicity(thy)); // 10
    report.extend(checks::fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs(thy)); // 11
    report.extend(checks::diff_left_right(thy)); // 12, 13 (diff mode only)
    report.extend(checks::formula_terms(thy)); // 14
    report.extend(checks::formula_guardedness(thy)); // 15
    report.extend(checks::lemma_annotations(thy)); // 16
    report.extend(checks::multiplication_restriction(thy)); // 17
    report.extend(checks::nat_sorts(thy)); // 18
    report.extend(checks::subterm_convergence(thy)); // 19

    let pn = checks::public_names_report(thy); // 3
    let anchors = after_public_names_topics();
    insert_wf_before(&mut report, pn, &anchors);
    report
}

term is then reported (not the offending subterm). Body:
    "  {Entity} `{name}' uses terms of the wrong form: {termlist}\n  \n" + fixed_help
Entity = `Lemma` or `Restriction`. Terms are collected in source order and are
NOT deduplicated (`x=y & x=y` -> `Free y', `Free y'`). Reducibility is decided by
the CALLER, which supplies a set of reducible function-symbol names; the checker
only consumes it (entry point `formula_terms_reducible(thy, &reducible)`;
`formula_terms(thy)` = the empty-set convenience wrapper - free variables only).

Raw term rendering (each term wrapped in `` `...' ``):
  - bound variable   -> `Bound N`   (de Bruijn: 0 = innermost binder; EVERY
                                      quantified var, incl. temporals, pushes one)
  - free variable    -> `Free <pp_var>`  (keeps the sort prefix, e.g. `Free #j`)
  - function app      -> `f(a,b)`   (args comma-joined, NO space)
  - tuple             -> `pair(a,pair(b,c))`  (right-nested binary pairs)
  - public constant   -> `'name'`
De Bruijn evidence: `All x y #i. h(x,y)` -> `h(Bound 2,Bound 1)`; nesting adds
depth (`All x #i.(Ex y #j. h(x))` -> `h(Bound 3)`). Free-inside-term evidence:
`f(y)` (f non-reducible, y free) -> `f(Free y)`; `f(h(x))` -> `f(h(Bound 1))`.

begun after the header (`  {Entity} `{name}' uses terms of the wrong form:`): a
token (term + trailing comma, except the last) stays on the line while

to line 2; an over-wide single term also wraps to line 2. Multiple wrong lemmas:

input.new_string:
### Formula guardedness (topic has LEADING space)  (round3: two modes + wrapping)
Trigger: a LEMMA formula not convertible to guarded form. (An unguarded
RESTRICTION is instead a FATAL error, not a warning -- observed z1; lemmas only.)
Body:
    "  Lemma `{name}' cannot be converted to a guarded formula:\n    {reason}\n      \"<sub>\"\n    in the formula\n      \"<whole>\""
where <sub> is the FAILING QUANTIFIER SUBTREE (not the whole formula) and <whole>
is the full lemma formula. Two observed reasons (probe r3_gc):
  - `unguarded variable(s) '#j', ... in the subformula`  (quantifier binds vars
    not guarded by an action fact)
  - `universal quantifier without toplevel implication`
Guardedness decision (∀): the body must be a top-level `guard ==> rest`; its
ANTECEDENT's action facts (temporal incl.) must bind every quantified var
(consequent does NOT guard). A conjunction/disjunction/negation/atom body -> "no
toplevel implication". A guarded ∀ recurses into antecedent and consequent
(finds nested failures; the reported subformula is the inner failing quantifier).
∃ takes a conjunctive guard (`Ex x #i. A(x)@#i` is fine); an ∃ with unguarded
vars is "unguarded variable(s)".

<sub>/<whole> use the multi-line formula printer (pp_formula_wrapped): a small
HughesPJ-style engine at page width ~72 (single line fits at total col 72, breaks
by 74). Rules (calibrated r3_gw/r3_qm/r3_and):
  - atom -> single-line text (never breaks); connective operands always parens'd.
  - binary op `(a) OP (b)`: breaks after OP; the right operand hangs at the
    connective's START COLUMN (the column just after its enclosing `(`).
  - quantifier `Q vars. body`: breaks after the `.`; body hangs at base+2
    (base = the quote indent 6, so body at col 8).
The formula is embedded as `      "..."` (quote col 6, formula col 7). When a
formula fits on one line the printer is byte-identical to the single-line
`pp_formula`, so narrow-formula fixtures are unaffected.

# Round 3 report (Unit C) — wellformedness clean-room

Scope: complete the wellformedness surface. All behavior re-derived by probing
`oracle/wf_oracle.sh`; no tamarin-rs source was read. Baseline 37 tests → 53
(16 new; 12 new byte-parity fixtures re-verified against the live oracle).

## 1. Formula terms — FULL coverage (reducible functions, de Bruijn)

Rewrote the check from free-variable collection to a TERM-based model. The unit
checked is each argument term of each atom (Eq etc. → both sides; Action → the
temporal FIRST then the fact args; Pred/Last → args). A term is "of the wrong
form" iff it contains a free variable OR a reducible function symbol; the WHOLE
top-level term is reported. New entry point
`formula_terms_reducible(thy, &BTreeSet<String>)` consumes a caller-computed set
of reducible symbol names (`formula_terms` is the empty-set wrapper;
`check_theory_with_reducible` threads it through the pipeline).

Raw term render (`show_wf_term`): bound var → `Bound N` (de Bruijn, 0 = innermost
binder, every quantified var incl. temporals pushes one); free var →
`Free <pp_var>`; app → `f(a,b)` (comma, NO space); tuple → `pair(a,pair(b,c))`;
pub const → `'name'`. Not deduplicated; source order. Term list is a fillSep at
column 69 (4-space continuation), begun after the header — a long name or a
single over-wide term can push the list to line 2. Multiple lemmas join by
`\n  \n`. Fixtures: r3_reducible, r3_freenest, r3_wrap6, r3_pair, r3_twolem.

## 2. Formula guardedness — two modes, nested selection, multi-line wrapping

- Nested-subformula selection: the reported `<sub>` is the FAILING QUANTIFIER
  SUBTREE, not the whole formula (`find_guard_failure` already returns it;
  fixture r3_guard_nested via `not (All …)`).
- Discovered and implemented a second failure reason: `universal quantifier
  without toplevel implication`. Decision (probe r3_gc): a ∀ body must be a
  top-level `guard ==> rest` whose ANTECEDENT actions bind every quantified var
  (consequent does not guard); any conjunction/disjunction/negation/atom body
  fails as "no toplevel implication". ∃ takes a conjunctive guard.
- Multi-line formula printer (`pp_formula_wrapped`): a compact HughesPJ-style
  engine at page width ~72. A binary connective breaks after its operator with
  the right operand hanging at its enclosing `(` column; a quantifier breaks
  after the `.` with its body hanging at base+2. Byte-exact on the implies-break
  (r3_guard_wide) and And-break (r3_guard_and) cases; identical to the
  single-line printer when a formula fits, so narrow fixtures are unaffected.

## 3. Fact capitalization issues (new topic, before Fact arity)

`Send` vs `SEND` across rules → a new topic laid out like Fact arity but listing
EVERY occurrence (no per-(rule,cap) dedup) with item text

- **Fresh-const list wrapping** (r3_fresh_wrap): now the same fillSep(69).
- **Multi-group public-names** separator (r3_pn_multi): `\n  \n` (was already
  correct; now fixtured).

## Residual gap (documented, not closed)

- **Rule-printer wide wrapping** (item 4b): a wide rule wraps to `[` / facts-
  fillSep / `]`, `-->` on its own line, header at indent 4 — and in the
  Multiplication context is co-printed with the out-of-scope Maude derivation
  reprint. A dedicated rule-layout engine is future work; the single-line rule
  printer stays byte-exact for short rules (all current fixtures). Structure
  recorded in BEHAVIOR.md / QUERIES.log (r3_rulewrap).

OUT OF SCOPE (per spec): Message Derivation / Derivation Checks.
